questions.question: In the following question, words are transformed by rearranging its letters according to a specific pattern. Apply the same patter to the fifth word and choose the correct answer from the given alternatives. TRAIL : TRLIA :: STEAL : TSLEA :: RATIO : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. TROIA. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Kavita_Paid%20mock%202_18.02.2026_D1.png)

questions.question: Statements: The university is planning to introduce a mandatory internship program for all the final year students. Assumptions: 1. Most of the students currently do not get internship opportunities. 2. Industry partners are willing to take interns.
options: Neither assumption 1 nor assumption 2 is implicit.
Both assumption 1 and assumption 2 are implicit.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Assumption 2 is implicit. Assumption 1: The decision to make internships mandatory does not necessarily mean students currently lack opportunities. It could simply be a strategic move to improve the quality of education or to ensure uniform practical exposure for everyone, regardless of their current access. Assumption 2: Whenever an organization or university introduces a mandatory policy (like an internship program), it inherently assumes that the necessary infrastructure or external cooperation required to execute that policy exists. For the program to be successful, the university must assume that industries are willing to accommodate these students. Assumption 2 is implicit. Hence, option 2 is correct.

questions.question: A university’s online exam server crashed during the final year exams affecting 50,000 students. What should the university administration do?
options: Postpone the exam indefinitely until a full audit is done
Conduct an offline based backup exam for all the students
Provide a grace period and re-conduct the exam for affected students only
Cancel the exam and give grades based on previous semester performance
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Provide a grace period and re-conduct the exam for affected students only. This is the most balanced and fair approach. It addresses the technical failure without punishing students who were not affected or delaying the entire academic calendar indefinitely. Providing a grace period allows students to recover from the stress of the technical glitch, while re-conducting the exam ensures the academic integrity of the final year results.

questions.question: In the question, a group of three numbers/symbols is given in each option. Identify the group that does NOT follow the same pattern as the others:
Compare the following two addresses: 1. Mr. Suresh Tiwari, C-12, Greenfield Colony, Hyderabad-500032 2. Mr. Suresh Tiwari, C-12, Greenfield Colony, Hyderabad – 500032
options: All the major details are matching.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. All the major details are matching. There is nothing much difference between the two addresses and all the major details are matching. Hence, option 1 is correct.

questions.question: If two days after today is Wednesday, then what day of the week was 673 days ago?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Sunday. Today + 2 → Wednesday Today → Monday 673/7 → remainder is 1 Monday - 1 day = Sunday

questions.question: In a certain coded language “program will start” is coded as “px af gt” and “guests will arrive” is coded as “sb gt ld”. What will be the code for “will” in that language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. gt. From "program will start" → "px af gt" and "guests will arrive" → "sb gt ld", The only common word is "will" and the only common code is "gt", So "will" = "gt"

questions.question: In the expression: A − B × C ÷ D + E, how is B related to E?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Mother’s Mother. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/RP2.18.1.png) B is mother’s mother of E

questions.question: Eight boxes: B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, B8 are kept one above another but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept between B6 and B3. B8 is kept immediately above B6. Only two boxes are kept between B8 and B4. B5 is kept below B4 but is not kept adjacent to B3. B7 is kept two boxes above B2. B1 is kept above B4. How many boxes are kept between B4 and B3?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 5 boxes. B3 B1 B7 B8 B6 B2 B4 B5 5 boxes are kept between B4 and B3.

questions.question: Following a certain logic 684 is related to 198 and 793 is related 396 . Following the same logic what is 564 related to? (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 99. \(684\Rightarrow486\) (Interchange the 1st and 3rd digits to make other number) 684 – 486 = 198 \(793 \Rightarrow 397\) 793 – 397 = 396 \(564 \Rightarrow 465\) 564 – 465 = 99 Option 1 is correct.

questions.question: A group of friends went for dinner and planned to split Rs. 832 equally. However, 3 didn't pay, due to which, each of the rest paid Rs. 12 extra to complete the payment. Find the total number of friends in the group.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 16. Let there be x friends and share of each is y originally. ATQ, xy = 832 ---- (i) But, 3 didn't pay and each of the rest paid Rs. 12 extra, So, (x-3)(y+12) = 832 So, xy = xy – 3y + 12x – 36 4x – 12 = y Putting this value in (i) x(4x – 12) = 832 ⇒ x(x – 3) = 208 or, x = 16 Hence, option 2 is correct

questions.question: Statements: National Aluminium Company has moved India from a position of shortage to self-sufficiency in the metal. Conclusions: I. Previously, India had to import aluminium. II. With this speed, it can soon become a foreign exchange earner.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only conclusion I follows. Conclusion I follows: The statement mentions a transition from a "position of shortage" to self-sufficiency. If a country faces a shortage of an essential metal like aluminum, it logically implies that it was required to import it to meet its domestic needs. Conclusion II does not follow: The statement focuses strictly on achieving self-sufficiency. The information provided does not mention production rates, future growth, or export capacity. This conclusion is a speculative.

questions.question: In a certain coded language EXODUS is coded as GZQFWU and LESSON is coded as NGUUQP. What will be the code for ANIMAL in the same coded language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. CPKOCN. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp2.13.1.png) Option 2 is correct

questions.question: Statement: P ≥ F = L ≥ B = Z = K = H ≥ X Conclusion: I. P ≥ K II. K
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only I follows. Statement: P ≥ F = L ≥ B = Z = K = H ≥ X Conclusion: I. P ≥ K → True (P ≥ F = L ≥ B = Z = K; P ≥ K) II. K False (P ≥ F = L ≥ B = Z = K; P ≥ K) Hence, conclusion I is true.

questions.question: Three of the four letter clusters are alike in some manner and hence form a group. Identify the letter cluster which does not belong to the group?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. LQU. YDG → Y + 5 → D + 3 → G LQU → L + 5 → Q + 3 → T ≠ U RWZ → R + 5 → W + 3 → Z BGJ → B + 5 → G + 3 → J

questions.question: If all the letters of the word PLASMODIUM are arranged in alphabetical order, how many letters will be there in the English alphabetical order between the letter that is third from the right end and the one that is second from the left end of the new word formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Eleven. P L A S M O D I U M \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) \(\downarrow\) A (D) I L M M O (P) S U Third letter from the right end = P Second letter from the left end = D Letters in between D and P = 11

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 27, 61, 108, 170, 249, ?
In a row of 25 students Dhiraj is ranked 12th from the bottom. What is the rank of Dhiraj from the top of the row?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 14th. Dhiraj = 12th from bottom Dhiraj from top = 25 - 12 + 1= 14th

questions.question: Statements: All Cats are Dogs. Some Dogs are Wolves. All Wolves are Mermaids. Conclusions: I. Some Dogs are Mermaids. II. No Cat is a Wolf. III. All Cats are Mermaids.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only conclusion I follows. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/RP2.5.1.png) Only conclusion I follows.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term Venezuela : Angel Falls :: Switzerland : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Rhine Falls. Angel falls is in Venezuela. Rhine falls is in Switzerland.

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that, when placed from left to right in the following blanks, will complete the letter-series. FP_B_PFPL_M_ _PL_MP
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. LMBPFB. F P L B M P | F P L B M P | F P L B M P

questions.question: If a # b = 41 and a # b = a² + ab – b² . Determine the value of a and b out of the following four pairs.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 7, 8. Putting the values from the option. We get a = 7 and b = 8 a # b = a2 + ab – b2 = 49 + 56 – 64 = 105 – 64 = 41

questions.question: Which of the following letter cluster will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? LEFT, PIJX, TMNB, XQRF, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. BUVJ. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/RP2.3.1.png)

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the sixth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the fourth term is related to the third term. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed) 26 : 338 :: 38 : 722 :: ? : 968
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 44. Pattern = \(a∶\frac{a^2}{2}\) \({26}^2\rightarrow\frac{676}{2}\rightarrow338\) \({38}^2\rightarrow\frac{1444}{2}\rightarrow722 \) \({44}^2\rightarrow\frac{1936}{2}\rightarrow968\ \mathrm{\ } \)

questions.question: What will come in place of ‘?’ if the symbols ‘÷’ and ‘−‘ are interchanged and ‘×’ and ‘+’ are interchanged? 36 × 21 + 8 ÷ 64 − 4 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 188. 36 × 21 + 8 ÷ 64 − 4 = ? Interchange signs ( ÷ ⇔ - ) & ( × ⇔ + ) 36 + (21 × 8) - (64 ÷ 4) = (36 + 168) - 16 = 204 - 16 = 188

questions.question: Consider the following statements and Select the correct statement • If speed of rotation of the earth increases, the weight of the body decreases. • The motions of a simple pendulum is an example of Uniform Motion.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Only 1. Statement – 1 Correct: If the speed of rotation of the earth increases then the weight of the body will decreases. Statement – 2 Incorrect: A simple pendulum consists of a bob hang by a string from a fixed support. It typically hangs vertically in its equilibrium position. When the bob is displaced from equilibrium and then released, it begins its back and forth vibration about its fixed equilibrium position. The motion is regular and repeating, an example of periodic motion.

questions.question: The 14th naval exercise 'INDRA-2025' was started between India and which country?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Russia. The INDRA naval exercise is a bilateral military exercise conducted between India and Russia. It covers all three services: Army, Navy, and Air Force.

questions.question: Who among the following described the Revolt of 1857 as a conspiracy?
options: Sir James Outram and W. Taylor
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Sir James Outram and W. Taylor. Sir James Outram and W. Taylor considered the 1857 Revolt to be a pre-planned conspiracy. They believed that the uprising was not just a random military mutiny but had elements of deliberate coordination, especially among Indian leaders and soldiers. This viewpoint differed from others: T.R. Holmes: Described it as a Muslim conspiracy to uproot British rule. Sir John Kay: Called it a sepoy mutiny, largely military in nature. Sir John Lawrence: Saw it as a series of local disturbances, not a unified plan.

questions.question: Consider the following statements and select the correct one: • Chlorophyll was first isolated and named by Caventou and Pelletier. • Christiaan Barnard is famous for delivering the world’s first test-tube baby. • General anaesthesia was invented by Hanaoka Seishu.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Only 1 and 3. Statement 1: Correct: Chlorophyll was first isolated and named by Caventou and Pelletier in 1817. The presence of Mg in chlorophyll discovered in 1906 and that was elements first detection in living tissue. Statement 2: Incorrect: Christiaan Barnard led the surgical team that performed the first human to human heart transplant in 1967. The first test-tube baby, Louise Brown was delivered by gynaecologist Patrick Steptoe and Robert Edwards in 1978. Statement 3: Correct: General anaesthesia was invented by Hanaoka Seishu, Japanese surgeon. He had great knowledge about Chinese herbal medicine, as well as Western surgical techniques.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding certain provisions of the Constitution of India: • Article 131 empowers the Supreme Court to adjudicate disputes between the Centre and one or more States, but it cannot be invoked in cases involving pre-Constitution treaties or agreements. • Article 139A empowers the Supreme Court to transfer certain cases from one High Court to another or from High Court to itself if substantial questions of constitutional law are involved. • Article 263 provides for an Inter-State Council which is a permanent constitutional body with mandatory regular meetings. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 1 and 2 only. Statement-1 Correct:- Under Article 131, the SC has original jurisdiction in Centre-State disputes. However, it does not cover matters arising out of pre-Constitution treaties, especially if excluded by Article 363. Statement-2 Correct: Article 139A, inserted by the 42nd Amendment, allows the SC to transfer cases involving the same substantial constitutional question from one High Court to another, or from HC to SC. Statement-3 Incorrect: Article 263 allows the President to establish an Inter-State Council, but it is not a permanent body, and its meetings are not mandatory unless directed. It is discretionary.

questions.question: What were the silver coins of the Gupta period called?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Rupaka. During the Gupta period, gold coins were called Dinar, modeled after Roman coins. Silver coins were known as Rupaka. Karshapan and Nishka were used in earlier periods [Mauryan and Vedic times respectively]. So, the silver currency of the Gupta era was known as Rupaka.

questions.question: Match the following: River Tributaries A) Godavari 1) Kabini B) Krishna 2) Indravati C) Brahmaputra 3) Bhima D) Kaveri 4) Lohit
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. A–2, B–3, C–4, D–1. • Indravati is a major tributary of the Godavari. • Bhima is an important tributary of the Krishna. • Lohit is a major tributary of the Brahmaputra. • Kabini is a tributary of the Kaveri.

questions.question: Who has been awarded the 'Shakti Bhatt Prize 2025' for the book 'The Lucky Ones'?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Zara Chowdhary. Zara Chowdhary has been awarded the Shakti Bhatt Prize 2025 for her debut book The Lucky Ones. The book blends memoir, oral history, and fiction, focusing on post-Partition experiences. The Shakti Bhatt Prize is a prestigious Indian literary award given to emerging writers for their debut works.

questions.question: According to Keynes, the speculative demand for money is primarily determined by:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Expectations of future interest rates. According to John Maynard Keynes, the speculative demand for money arises from the desire to hold cash instead of interest-bearing assets [like bonds], based on one's expectations about future interest rates.

questions.question: With reference to no-confidence motion, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?
options: If it is passed in the Lok Sabha, the council of ministers must resign from office.
It is moved for ascertaining the confidence of Lok Sabha in the council of ministers.
It can be moved against the entire council of ministers only.
It should state the reasons for its adoption in the Lok Sabha.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. It should state the reasons for its adoption in the Lok Sabha. No confidence motion – It can be introduced only in Lok Sabha and admitted for discussion when at least 50 members supports the motions in the house.

questions.question: The mass of proton and mass of ………is same.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Neutron. A proton is a positively charged particle which is found in the core of atom with neutrons in the nucleus. Its mass is 1.672 × 10-27 kg, which is approximately 1837 times the mass of the electron The mass of both protons and neutrons is approximately equal. The proton unit is electrically charged while neutron is neutral.

questions.question: D. K. Pattammal was a legendary figure in Indian classical music. She was primarily associated with which musical instrument or style?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Vocal [Carnatic]. D. K. Pattammal [Damal Krishnaswamy Pattammal] was one of the legendary “Female Trinity” of Carnatic music along with M. S. Subbulakshmi and M. L. Vasanthakumari. She was a vocalist, not an instrumentalist, and was known for her mastery in ragam-tanam-pallavi, patriotic songs, and devotional compositions. She was the first Brahmin woman to publicly perform Carnatic music concerts, breaking social barriers.

questions.question: Consider the following statements and select the correct statement/statements. • The office of the Vijarat reached its peak under the Khilji dynasty. • Balban’s tomb was the first tomb in India that built in the pure Islamic style.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Only 2. Statement 1: Incorrect: The office of the vizier reached its peak during the Tughlaq period. Especially during the reign of Firoz Shah Tughlaq, the position of vizier attained its highest prominence. Statement 2: Correct: In terms of architectural elements like the true arch and dome, Balban’s tomb is considered the first example in India of construction using pure Islamic architectural principles.

questions.question: Kalighat Pat art is related to which state?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. West Bengal. Kalighat Pat Art is a traditional style of painting that originated in the 19th century in the vicinity of the Kalighat Kali Temple in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Statement [A]: The Cripps Mission failed because it proposed the immediate transfer of power to Indian leaders. Reason [R]: Indian leaders rejected the offer as the British retained control over defence and failed to provide a clear commitment to independence. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options: [A] and [R] are true, and [R] is the correct explanation of [A].
[A] and [R] are true, but [R] is not the correct explanation of [A].
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. [A] is false, but [R] is true. The Cripps Mission (1942) did not propose immediate transfer of power — it offered dominion status after the war and allowed provinces the choice to opt out, which was deeply problematic for Indian unity. So, Statement A is false. However, Reason R is true — Indian leaders, including the Congress and the Muslim League, rejected the proposal because: Defence was to remain under British control. There was no real commitment to immediate independence and the offer allowed partition in principle.

questions.question: Which statement is true about the temperate zone? • They lie between Tropic of Cancer and Arctic Circle in Northern Hemisphere and Tropic of Capricorn and Antarctic Circle in Southern Hemisphere. • They have extreme cold climate.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Only 1 is correct. Statement 1: Correct – The temperate zones are indeed located between: • Northern Hemisphere: Tropic of Cancer [23.5°N] and Arctic Circle [66.5°N] • Southern Hemisphere: Tropic of Capricorn [23.5°S] and Antarctic Circle [66.5°S] Statement 2: Incorrect – The temperate zones do not have an extreme cold climate. • They experience moderate climate with distinct seasons [summer, winter, spring, autumn]. • Extreme cold is characteristic of the polar zones, not temperate zones.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about diseases is/are correct? • Malaria is caused by a protozoan parasite transmitted by female Anopheles mosquitoes. • Tuberculosis is caused by a bacterium called Mycobacterium Tuberculosis. • Diabetes is caused by a virus that reduces insulin production in the body.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Only 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 1: Correct – Malaria is caused by Plasmodium (a protozoan parasite) transmitted by the bite of an infected female Anopheles mosquito. Statement 2: Correct – Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, a bacterial pathogen. Statement 3: Incorrect – Diabetes (especially Type 1) is not caused by a virus; it’s due to an autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing cells or insulin resistance (in Type 2).

questions.question: Match the following: Festival State/Region A) Chapchar Kut 1) Nagaland B) Wangala 2) Mizoram C) Myoko 3) Meghalaya D) Sekrenyi 4) Arunachal Pradesh
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. A-2, B-3, C-4, D-1. Chapchar Kut is a major harvest festival of Mizoram. Wangala (100 Drums Festival) is celebrated by the Garo tribe of Meghalaya. Myoko is a traditional festival of the Apatani tribe in Arunachal Pradesh. Sekrenyi is an important festival of the Angami tribe in Nagaland.

questions.question: Choose the correct pair from the following options:
options: Third Five-year Plan – Rapid industrialisation and basic industries
Fourth Five-year Plan – Family planning programme
Second Five-year Plan – Focus on agriculture
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Fourth Five-year Plan – Family planning programme. • Third Five-year Plan – Rapid industrialization and basic industries → Incorrect. The Third Plan (1961–66) focused on agriculture and aiming to achieve self-sufficiency in food grains, not primarily on rapid industrialization (which was Second Plan’s focus). • Fourth Five-year Plan – Family planning programme → Correct. The Fourth Plan (1969–74) gave special emphasis to family planning as a means of population control. • First Five-year Plan – Mahalanobis model → Incorrect. The First Plan (1951–56) was based on the Harrod–Domar model, not the Mahalanobis model. The Mahalanobis model was used in the Second Plan. • Second Five-year Plan – Focus on agriculture → Incorrect The Second Plan (1956–61) focused on rapid industrialization with the Mahalanobis model, not agriculture.

questions.question: Identify the mineral on the basis of the following characteristics relating to it: I. It is usually a greenish crystal. II. It is often found in basaltic rocks. III. Magnesium, iron and silica are its major elements.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Olivine. Olivine is typically greenish in color, often appearing as transparent to translucent crystals. It is a primary mineral in basaltic and ultramafic rocks. Chemically, olivine is a magnesium iron silicate, hence contains magnesium, iron, and silica as its main elements.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the first transwoman Travelling Ticket Examiner (TTE) of Southern Railway in 2024?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Sindhu Ganapathy. Sindhu Ganapathy was appointed as the first transwoman Travelling Ticket Examiner (TTE) in the Southern Railway.

questions.question: The Gandhi–Irwin Pact included which of the following? • Invitation to Congress to participate in the Round Table Conference. • Withdrawal of Ordinances promulgated in connection with the Civil Disobedience Movement. • Release of only those prisoners who were not charged with violence. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 1, 2 and 3. The Gandhi–Irwin Pact (1931) was an agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and the then Viceroy, Lord Irwin, which aimed to end the Civil Disobedience Movement. The main terms included: • Invitation to Congress to participate in the Second Round Table Conference. • Withdrawal of repressive ordinances issued in connection with the Civil Disobedience Movement. • Release of political prisoners who had not been charged with violence.

questions.question: Saline soils are also known as:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Usara soil. Saline soils are also known as Usara Soils. Saline soils contain large amounts of water-soluble salts that inhibit seed germination and plant growth. These soils originate from the natural weathering of minerals. Sodium, Potassium and Magnesium are present in abundant amount in these types of soils. The pH value of this soils is 8.5.

questions.question: Which city is situated on the banks of Gomti River?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Lucknow. Lucknow is situated on the banks of the Gomti River, a tributary of the Ganga.

questions.question: The 24th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1971, was enacted primarily to:
options: Empower the President to amend Fundamental Rights without Parliament’s approval
Explicitly affirm Parliament’s power to amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights, and make Presidential assent to such bills mandatory
Restrict Parliament from amending the Basic Structure of the Constitution
Grant Parliament the power to amend only Directive Principles of State Policy without affecting Fundamental Rights
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Explicitly affirm Parliament’s power to amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights, and make Presidential assent to such bills mandatory. The 24th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1971 was enacted in response to the Supreme Court’s Golaknath case (1967), which had held that Parliament could not amend Fundamental Rights. This amendment: • Amended Article 368 to clearly state that Parliament has the power to amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights. • Made Presidential assent compulsory for a Constitution Amendment Bill. It did not talk about the Basic Structure doctrine (which came later in Kesavananda Bharati case, 1973).

questions.question: In a ∆ABC, right angled at A, if tan B = \(\frac{9}{40} \), then find \(\frac{{tan}^2A-\ {sec}^2A}{1-{cos}^2B} \).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(\frac{-1681}{81}\). ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/MP2.1.png) \(\frac{{tan}^2A-\ {sec}^2A}{1-{cos}^2B} = \frac{-1}{{sin}^2B} = {-cosec}^2B=\ {-(\frac{41}{9})}^2 = \frac{-1681}{81}\)

questions.question: The profit made on an item sold for Rs. 2160 is equal to the loss incurred when it is sold for Rs. 1440. What will be the profit or loss percentage when it is sold for Rs. 1944?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 8%. 2160 – CP = CP – 1440 \(\Rightarrow\) CP = Rs. 1800 Profit % when SP is Rs. 1944 = \(\frac{144}{1800}×100\) = 8%

questions.question: In a triangle ABC, ∠A = 89°. The perpendicular bisector of AB at D meets BC at E. If ∠AED = 43° what is the measure of ∠C?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 44°. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/MP2.2.png) Given that DE is perpendicular bisector o AB. So, AD = BD, ∠ADE = ∠BDE = 90° and DE is common Hence, ∆ADE ≅ ∆BDE. Therefore, ∠AED = ∠BED = 43° So, ∠DBE = 90° – 43° = 47° Hence, ∠C = 180° – (∠A+∠B) = 180° – (89° + 47°) = 44°

questions.question: The compound interest on a certain sum in 2\(\frac{1}{2}\) years at 8% p.a., interest compounded yearly, is Rs 6658. Find the sum?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Rs. 31250. Principal : Amount 25 : 27 25 : 27 25 : 26 15625 : 18954 Interest Earned = 3329→ 6658 So, Principal = \(\frac{6658}{3329}\)×15625 = Rs. 31250

questions.question: If (2x3 + ax2 + 2x + 4) is exactly divisible by (x + 2). Find the value of a?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 4. As, it is given that given (2x3 + ax2 + 2x + 4) is exactly divisible by (x+2); i.e. remainder = 0 Hence, 2(–2)3 + a(–2)2 + 2×(–2) + 4 = 0 \(\Rightarrow\) a = 4

questions.question: A thief committed a crime and escaped from the spot at a speed of 24 km/h. A security guard started chasing him 30 minutes after the thief started running and caught him in the next 10 minutes. What is the speed (in km/h) of the security guard?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 96. Ratio of time taken by the thief and the security guard to cover the same distance = 40 : 10 = 4:1 Therefore, Speed ratio (Thief : Security Guard) = 1:4 Hence, Speed of the Security Guard = 24×4 = 96 km/h

questions.question: A pyramid has an equilateral base of length 10cm. The slant height of the pyramid is 10 cm. Find the volume of the pyramid (in cm3).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. \(\frac{125}{3}\sqrt{11}\). ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/MP2.3.png) h= \(\sqrt{{10}^2-\left(\frac{10}{2\sqrt3}\right)^2}\) =\(\sqrt{100-\frac{100}{12}}=\sqrt{\frac{1100}{12}}=\frac{10}{2}\sqrt{\frac{11}{3}}\) V= \(\frac{1}{3}\times\frac{\sqrt3}{4}\times10\times10\times\frac{10}{2}\frac{\sqrt{11}}{\sqrt3}=\frac{125}{3}\sqrt{11}\ cm^3\)

questions.question: How many numbers between 10 and 110 are divisible by both 2 and 5 but not by 3?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 6. 2 and 5 \(\rightarrow\) must be div. long. 10- ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/MP2.4.png) 2 and 5 but not by -3 → ( 9 – 3 ) = 6 number.

questions.question: Rs. 1400 were distributed among A, B, C and D in such a way that C and D together get 3/4th of what A and B together gets. C gets 9/4 th of B and D gets 1/3 rd of C. Find the share of B?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Rs. 200. C + D : A + B = 3 : 4 C : B = 9 : 4 D : C = 1 : 3 = 3 : 9 So, C + D : A + B = 12 : 16 C : B : D 9 : 4 : 3 A : B 12 : 4 C : D 9 : 3 Share of B = \(\frac{4}{28}\times1400=200\)

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an LED at Rs. x and earns a profit of 25%. On the occasion of Deepawali, he marks the same LED at Rs. 1.5x and offers a discount of 20%. What will be the profit percentage that he will make during the Deepawali offer?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 50%. Let, x = Rs. 50, then CP of LED = Rs. 40 New Marked Price on Deepawali = 50×1.5 = Rs. 75 And New SP = 75×0.8 = Rs. 60 So, Profit % during Deepawali offer = \(\frac{(60-40)}{40}\)×100 = 50%

questions.question: If the mode of the following data is 15, find the value of k. 11, 12, 8, 9, k, 11, 15, 12, 14, 15
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 15. 11, 12, 8, 9, k, 11, 15, 12, 14, 15 In the given above data, all three numbers 11, 12 and 15 occur 2 times and also given that 15 is mode of the given data; i.e. 15 occurs maximum number of times. Therefore, k = 15. Now, 15 will occur three times; maximum times of all.

questions.question: Two circles intersect each other at two distinct points. Which of the following statements is always true?
options: The external tangent is longer than the internal tangent
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Exactly two common tangents exist. When two circles intersect at two distinct points, they overlap in a way that prevents any tangent from passing between them. So, There are exactly two external tangents that touch both circles without passing between their centers.

questions.question: A can do \(\frac{1}{4}\) part of a work in 9 days. B can do \(\frac{2}{3}\) part of the same work in 28 days. Working together, in how many days can A and B complete the whole work?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 252/13 days. A can do \(\frac{1}{4}\) part of a work in 9 days ∴ A will do whole work in = \(9\times4=36\ days\) ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/MP2.5.png) \(\frac{252}{\left(7+6\right)}=\frac{252}{13}\) days

questions.question: Simplify the following: \(\frac{\sqrt{45}+\sqrt{605}-\sqrt{405}}{\sqrt{720}-\sqrt{245}} \)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 1. \(\frac{\sqrt{45}+\sqrt{605}-\sqrt{405}}{\sqrt{720}-\sqrt{245}}=\ \frac{3\sqrt5+11\sqrt5-9\sqrt5}{12\sqrt5-7\sqrt5} = \frac{5\sqrt5}{5\sqrt5}=1\ \)

questions.question: Average salary of A,B and C is Rs. 15,000 per month and average salary of B,C and D is 12,000 per month. If the salary of A is twice that of D, find the average salary of B and C?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Rs. 13,500. A+B+C=45,000 B+C+D=36,000 A-D=9000 2D-D=9000 D = 9000 B+C=36000-9000=27000 Average \(\left(B+C\right)=\frac{27000}{2}\) = 13,500

questions.question: A cube of 12cm edge is immersed completely in a rectangular vessel containing water. If the dimension of base area of the rectangle be 15cm and 12.5cm, find the rise in water level?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 9.216 cm. Volume of the cube = volume of water rose Let the rise in water level = x cm 12×12×12=15×12.5×x x = 9.216 cm

questions.question: The largest possible length of a tape which can measure 765 cm, 935 cm and 1275 cm length of clothes in a minimum number of attempts without measuring the length of a cloth in a fraction of the tape’s length?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 85 cm. To find the largest possible length of a tape that can measure three different lengths exactly (without fractions), we need to find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) or Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of the three given lengths. HCF of 765 cm, 935 cm and 1275 cm = 85 cm\ The largest possible length of the tape which can measure the clothes in a minimum number of attempts is 85 cm

questions.question: In a triangle PQR, the medians PM and QN intersect at the centroid G. If the length of median PM is 15 cm, what is the length of segment PG?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 10 cm. Medians intersect one another at centroid. And centroid divides medians in the ratio 2:1. Since, G is the centroid, PG:GM = 2:1 The length of PM = 3 units = 15 cm So, the length of PG = 2 units = 10 cm

questions.question: From a container of milk, a thief has stolen 10 litres of milk and replace it with same quantity of water. He repeated the same process 2 times more, thus the ratio of milk to water in the final mixture becomes 729:271. The initial amount of milk in the container was?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 100 lit. \(\frac{Milk\left(10r+\right)}{water\left(added\right)}=\frac{729}{271}\) \(\frac{milk\left(lit\right)}{water\left(initi\right)}=\frac{729}{1000}\) \(729x=1000x\left(1-\frac{10}{K}\right)^3\) K = 100lit

questions.question: Rohan needed ₹40,000 for expanding his business and divided the amount between two private lenders on simple interest: • Lender P charges 10% p.a. simple interest • Lender Q charges 7% p.a. simple interest Rohan kept both loans for 5 years and then made a single lump-sum payment to clear only the total interest, which amounted to ₹17,750. Later, he realized that if the principal amounts borrowed from the two lenders had been interchanged, his total interest payment over five years would have been ₹1,500 less than what he actually paid. How much money did Rohan borrow at 10% p.a. from Lender P?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. ₹25,000. Interest in 5 years = ₹17750, so, interest in 1 year = ₹3550 %age of interest rate = \(\frac{3550}{40000}\times100\) = 71/8 % P Q 10% 7% 71/8% 15 9 Ratio of principal of P:Q = 5:3 Hence, Rohan borrowed \(\frac{5}{8}\times40000\) = ₹25000 at 10% interest rate.

questions.question: Two circular cakes have radii in the ratio 3 : 5. If the area of the smaller cake is 180π cm², what is the area of the larger cake?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 500π cm². If the ratio of radii of two circle = 3 : 5 The ratio of their area = (3)² : (5)² = 9 : 25 Given that, area of smaller cake = 180π cm² So, the area of larger cake = \(\frac{180\pi}{9}\)×25 = 500π cm²

questions.question: A and B are partners in a business. They invest in the ratio 4:5, at the end of 6 months A withdraws from the business. If they receive profits in the ratio 6:5, find how long B’s investment was used?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 4 months. Ratio of investment = 4:5 A invests for 6 months and let B invests for x months. Ratio of profit = 6:5 Hence, \(\frac{4\times6}{5\times x}=\frac{6}{5}\)=> x = 4 months

questions.question: A vertical tower of height H stands on level ground. From a point A on the ground, the angle of elevation of the top of the tower is 45°. From another point B, which is 12 m away from the tower along the same straight line, the angle of elevation of the top of the tower is 30°. What is the height of the tower?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 4√3 m. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/MP2.6.png) If angle is 45°, ratio of side = 1:1:√2 and when angle is 30°, the ratio of side is 1:√3:2 H = OA = 1 unit And, OB = √3 unit = 12 m Hence, H = 1 unit = 4√3 m

questions.question: A is 25% less than B and C is 40% of the sum of A & B, then by what percentage is A greater than C?
If x= \(\frac{\sqrt{\sqrt5+2}}{\sqrt{\sqrt5-2}},\) then find the value of x2+x-11.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(5\sqrt5\). Given x= \(\frac{\sqrt{\sqrt5+2}}{\sqrt{\sqrt5-2}}\ \Rightarrow x= \frac{\sqrt{\left(\sqrt5+2\right)(\sqrt5+2)}}{\sqrt{\left(\sqrt5-2\right)(\sqrt5+2)}}\ \Rightarrow x=\ (\sqrt5+2)\) So, \(x^2+x-11 = {(\sqrt5+2)}^2 + \sqrt5+2 – 11 = 5\sqrt5\)

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: The captain along with his teammates,
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. No Error. This sentence is grammatically correct. ✅ Grammatical Concept: When the subject is followed by phrases such as “along with,” “as well as,” “together with,” “in addition to,” etc., the verb agrees with the main [first] subject only, not with the noun in the modifying phrase. • Here, the main subject is “The captain” [‘singular]. • “Along with his teammates” is an additional phrase and does not affect the verb. • Therefore, the singular verb “is scheduled” is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. By late November, the cold weather had ___________ and snow began to fall regularly.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. set in. Explanation: The sentence talks about cold weather beginning and continuing, especially by late November, which is a gradual and often unpleasant change. The phrasal verb that best fits this meaning is "set in." It means to begin and seem likely to continue, often used for weather, illness, or negative situations. Let’s see the meaning of all the key phrasal verbs: • Set in – शुरूहोजाना / असरदिखाना – To begin and seem likely to continue [often unpleasant]. Example: “Winter has set in early this year.” • Set out – रवानाहोना / शुरूकरना – To begin a journey or plan. Example: “They set out for their hike at sunrise.” •Set off – यात्राशुरूकरना / विस्फोटकरना – To start a journey or cause something [like an alarm or explosion] to occur. Example: “The fire alarm was set off by the smoke.” •Set up – स्थापितकरना / तैयारकरना – To arrange, build, or prepare something. Example: “They set up a tent in the forest.” Therefore, Option [3], set in, is the most logical and contextually accurate choice.

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no correction is needed, select ‘No correction required’. The cattles is grazing peacefully on the vast green pastures near the riverbank during the early morning hours.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. cattle are. Error Concept: Incorrect plural form and verb agreement with collective noun [From the chapter: Noun] 📚 Grammatical Concept: Cattle is a plural collective noun used for a group of animals and never takes an‘s’ at the end. • Using “cattles” is grammatically incorrect. • Since “cattle” refers to multiple animals, it takes a plural verb such as “are”, not the singular “is”. 🔧 How to improve it: • Remove the incorrect “s” from “cattles” to correctly write “cattle”. • Replace the singular verb “is” with the plural verb “are” to match the plural subject. ✔ Correct Sentence: The cattle are grazing peacefully on the vast green pastures near the riverbank during the early morning hours.

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence.
options: The supervisor might to assign additional tasks today.
She might handles the client interaction alone.
They might be completing the audit by evening.
The team might reconsider its earlier decision.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e.The team might reconsider its earlier decision. CONCEPT – Use of Modal Verb “Might” (Base Verb Rule) ✅ • “Might” is a modal verb used to express possibility, probability or weak certainty. • All modal verbs are followed by the base form (V1) of the main verb. • After modal verbs, we never use “to + verb” and never use verb forms like -s / -es / -ing / -ed. • Basic structure: Subject + modal verb + base verb (V1) Correct Sentence – “The team might reconsider its earlier decision.” ✅

questions.question: Identify the word that is incorrectly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Masaacre. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Reassert - पुनः जोर देना - To assert again or strongly Example: The company will reassert its dominance in the market with its new product. Mongrels - कुत्तों की मिश्रित नस्ल - Dogs of mixed breed Example: The family adopted a mongrel from the animal shelter. Massacre - नरसंहार - The killing of a large number of people or animals, often in a brutal or indiscriminate manner Example: The massacre in the village was condemned by the international community. Etiquette - शिष्टाचार - Rules of behaviour considered polite or proper Example: Learning etiquette is essential for making a good impression in formal settings.

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no correction is needed, select ‘No correction required’. He behaves as if he was the boss of this company.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. were the boss. Error Concept: Use of "was" in unreal/hypothetical condition [from the chapter: Subjunctive Mood] 📚Grammatical Concept: In English, when expressing imaginary, hypothetical, or unreal situations, especially after expressions like “as if,” “as though,” “if,” “I wish,” “would that”, we use the subjunctive form of the verb. • For all subjects [including singular ones like he, she, it], the verb "were" is used instead of "was" to indicate that the condition is not real or is contrary to fact. • In this sentence, the phrase "as if he was the boss" expresses an unreal or imaginary condition—he is not actually the boss. • Therefore, "was" is grammatically incorrect here. The correct subjunctive form is "were." 🔧How to improve it: Replace “was” with “were” to correctly express the hypothetical condition. ✔Correct Sentence: He behaves as if he were the boss of this company.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – We have been taught to conduct exhaustive studies of our chosen fields.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Intensive. Let’s know the meaning of all words: Exhaustive - विस्तृत और गहन - thorough and comprehensive Example: The researchers conducted an exhaustive study on the effects of climate change, analysing data from multiple sources. Perfunctory - ऊपरी - Done without much interest or care Example: He gave a perfunctory nod to acknowledge her presence. Intensive - गहन - Thorough and detailed, and concentrated Example: The team conducted an intensive study of the market trends to develop a successful strategy. Expel - निकालना - To force someone or something out Example: The student was expelled from school for violating the rules. Impeccable - निर्दोष - Completely faultless or flawless Example: Her impeccable manners made her a favourite among her peers.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Vague
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Apparent. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Vague - अस्पष्ट - Not clearly or precisely defined Example: The politician's vague promises failed to convince many voters. Veil - पर्दा - A piece of fabric that covers something Example: The bride wore a beautiful veil that complemented her wedding dress perfectly. Subside - शांत होना - To become less intense or severe Example: The doctor assured us that the patient's fever would subside with the medication, and they would start feeling better soon. Nebulous - अस्पष्ट - Unclear or vague in meaning Example: The artist's nebulous description of her inspiration made it difficult for the critics to understand the true meaning behind her masterpiece. Apparent - स्पष्ट - Clearly visible or understood; obvious Example: The apparent lack of interest in the project was contradicted by the team's enthusiastic discussion.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Jane hoped for a __________ in her energy after she took a nap.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Resurgence. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Pleasant - सुखद - Giving a sense of enjoyment or pleasure Example: The pleasant weather made the outdoor event enjoyable. Resurgence - पुनरुत्थान - A renewed growth or increase in activity or strength Example: The company experienced a resurgence in sales after launching a new marketing campaign. Kinship - संबंध - A feeling of being connected or related Example: The shared experience created a sense of kinship among the team members. Impost - कर - A tax or duty imposed on something Example: The government levied an impost on imported luxury goods.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. To keep body and soul together
options: To manage to live/to have just enough to sustain
Something that is impossible to get or achieve
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. To manage to live/to have just enough to sustain. Explanation: The idiom “to keep body and soul together” means to survive with great difficulty, having barely enough money, food, or resources to stay alive. It highlights a condition of extreme hardship where a person manages only the basic necessities of life. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from the idea that the body (physical existence) and the soul (life force) must remain together for a person to stay alive. If one cannot meet basic needs, even this minimal survival becomes difficult. Example: With such a low income, he struggles to keep body and soul together. Hindi: “to keep body and soul together” = किसी तरह गुज़ारा करना / मुश्किल से जीवन यापन करना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. She dares not to speak. Error Concept: Incorrect Use of Modal Verb [from the chapter Modal Auxiliaries] Grammatical Concept: When "dare" is used as a modal verb, especially in negative or interrogative forms, it follows the structure of other modal verbs: • It does not take -s in third person singular [e.g., "he/she dare", not "dares"]. • It is followed directly by the base form [V1] of the verb without "to". • “Dares not to speak” is incorrect as it wrongly treats “dare” as a regular verb and incorrectly uses the to-infinitive form. Correct Sentence: “She dare not speak against her supervisor during team meetings.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Conscript
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Muster. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Conscript - भर्ती करना - To forcibly enlist someone in the military Example - The government decided to conscript young men into the military to bolster the country's Defence forces. Brittle - भंगुर - Fragile or easily breakable Example: The brittle glass vase shattered into pieces when it fell. Debase - अपमानित करना - To reduce the value or quality of something Example: The company tried to debase the competitor's product through false advertising. Succinct - संक्षिप्त - Briefly and clearly expressed Example: The manager gave a succinct summary of the project's progress. Muster - इकट्ठा करना - To gather or assemble people, especially for military service; to collect or summon Example: The army will muster the troops for a grand parade to celebrate the national holiday.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The moribund industry struggled to survive amidst intense competition.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Resurgent. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Moribund - मरने के कगार पर - Approaching death or extinction Example - The moribund company was on the verge of bankruptcy due to years of financial struggles. Deteriorating - बिगड़ना - Becoming worse or declining Example: The deteriorating condition of the building required immediate repair. Jostle - धक्का देना - To push or shove someone or something Example: The crowd began to jostle each other to get a glimpse of the celebrity. Harrowing - अत्यधिक दुखदायी - Extremely distressing or disturbing Example: The harrowing experience of losing a loved one can be life-changing. Resurgent – पुनर्जीवित - Experiencing renewed growth or vitality Example: The company's resurgent sales figures indicated a successful marketing strategy.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. She was annoyed at __________ arriving late to the meeting.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. his. Error Concept: The error lies in using an object pronoun [like him] instead of a possessive adjective [his] before a gerund. Grammatical Concept: In English grammar, when a gerund [verb + ing used as a noun] is preceded by a noun or pronoun, it must be in the possessive form. This is because the gerund functions as a noun, and we show ownership or association using possessive adjectives [my, your, his, her, our, their]. The corrected sentence is: “She was annoyed at his arriving late to the meeting.”

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. To put off doing something, especially out of habitual carelessness or laziness
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Procrastinate. Let’s know the meaning of all words: Procrastinate – टालना - To delay or put off doing something, often due to habitual carelessness or laziness Example: She tends to procrastinate when it comes to completing tasks on time. Convalescence - स्वास्थ्यलाभ - The process of recovering from an illness or injury Example: After the surgery, she required a period of convalescence to recover fully. Slouch - ढीला लटकना - To sit or stand with a slumped posture Example: He tends to slouch when he's tired or bored. Oblivious - अनजान - Unaware or unconscious of something Example: She was oblivious to the fact that people were staring at her. to the fact that people were staring at her.)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Bend over backwards
options: Extremely hard to help or to please someone
Leave, typically to escape from or avoid someone or something
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Extremely hard to help or to please someone. Explanation: The idiom “bend over backwards” means to make a great and often excessive effort to help or satisfy someone. It conveys the idea of going far beyond what is normally expected in order to please or assist another person. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from the physical image of a person bending so far backward that it is difficult and uncomfortable, symbolizing an extraordinary level of effort. Example: She bent over backwards to make her guests feel comfortable. Hindi: “bend over backwards” = बहुत अधिक प्रयास करना / किसी को खुश करने के लिए हद से ज़्यादा कोशिश करना

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. To affect somebody in a way that makes them act /think in an immoral way
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Pervert. Let’s see the meaning of all the words: Tonic- टॉनिक - a medicine or drink that is supposed to improve one's health Example—The doctor prescribed a tonic to boost my energy levels. Pervert- विकृत करना - to influence or distort someone's thoughts or actions in a way that is considered immoral or unnatural Example—The explicit content perverted the young minds. Amnesia – स्मृतिलोप – loss of memory Example—After the accident, he suffered from amnesia and couldn't recall his past. Ode - स्तुति कविता - a poem expressing praise or admiration Example—The poet wrote an ode to nature, describing its beauty.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. Consequently, societies that fail to invest in critical thinking risk becoming vulnerable to misinformation. B. This shift demands that education systems move beyond rote memorization. C. The explosion of digital content has fundamentally changed how people consume information. D. Instead, greater emphasis must be placed on analysis, evaluation, and independent judgment.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. CBDA. Explanation: • C is the best opening sentence because it introduces the main cause — the explosion of digital content. • B logically follows since “This shift” refers to the change described in sentence C. • D comes next as it explains what should replace rote memorization. • A is the concluding sentence because “Consequently” summarizes the final outcome. ✅ Final paragraph: The explosion of digital content has fundamentally changed how people consume information. This shift demands that education systems move beyond rote memorization. Instead, greater emphasis must be placed on analysis, evaluation, and independent judgment. Consequently, societies that fail to invest in critical thinking risk becoming vulnerable to misinformation. 💡 Exam Tip : Always identify reference words (this, such, instead, consequently) and place cause before result. This strategy quickly eliminates wrong sequences in hard PQRS questions.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. We had to stop all other work to complete our assignment.
options: All other work has to be stopped by us to complete our assignment.
All other work had stopped by us to complete our assignment.
All other work had to be stopped by us to complete our assignment.
All other work was stopped by us to complete our assignment.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. All other work had to be stopped by us to complete our assignment. Explanation: The original sentence is in the past tense with a modal verb “had to”, which expresses obligation or necessity. When converting such sentences into passive voice, the following rules apply: • The object “all other work” in active voice becomes the subject in passive voice. • The modal phrase “had to stop” changes to “had to be stopped.” • The subject “we” changes to the agent introduced by “by us.” • The tense and modality must be preserved in passive form. Therefore, Option [3] correctly preserves the tense, modality, and voice, making it the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect/direct speech. The professor asked, “Why did you choose this particular topic for your research?”
options: The professor asked why I had chosen that particular topic for my research.
The professor asked why had I chosen that particular topic for my research.
The professor asked that why I chose this particular topic for my research.
The professor asked why I have chosen that particular topic for research.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. The professor asked why I had chosen that particular topic for my research. Explanation: The original sentence is a WH-question in the past tense. To convert it into indirect speech: • The reporting verb “asked” remains unchanged. • The question word “Why” stays at the beginning of the indirect question. • Question word order changes to statement form [no inversion: “why I had chosen” instead of “why had I chosen”]. • The past simple “did choose” becomes past perfect “had chosen.” • Pronouns and demonstratives are also adjusted: “you” → “I”, “this” → “that.” Direct: The professor asked, “Why did you choose this particular topic for your research?” Indirect: The professor asked why I had chosen that particular topic for my research. Hence, option [1] is the correct and most grammatically accurate transformation.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. operate. 💢 Explanation - The sentence refers to oral narratives and monuments functioning as repositories of shared identity. Operate means function or work in a particular way, which fits the idea that these cultural forms actively serve as carriers of memory. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 2 – fabricate means invent falsely, which contradicts preservation. →Option 3 – obscure means hide, opposite of preserving memory. →Option 4 – displace means remove or replace, not preserve.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. resilient. 💢 Explanation - The sentence says that although cultural practices evolved due to interaction among communities, certain traditions remained remarkably ____. This contrast shows that despite change and external influence, some traditions continue to survive and retain their essence. Resilient means having the ability to endure, adapt, and recover from change or pressure without losing core identity. In the context of history and culture, it highlights how deeply rooted traditions persist across generations, even when social, political, or cultural environments transform. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 1 – volatile means unstable and prone to frequent change. This contradicts the idea of traditions remaining constant over time. →Option 3 – transient means short-lived or temporary. The passage clearly suggests longevity, not short duration. →Option 4 – arbitrary means random or based on no fixed principle. Cultural traditions, however, are inherited and structured, not random.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. skew. 💢 Explanation - The sentence states: “when historical narratives are selectively presented, they can ____ collective understanding and distort cultural consciousness.” The key idea here is distortion caused by partial or biased presentation. "Skew" means to twist, distort, or bias something away from its true or balanced form. Selective history highlights only certain facts while ignoring others, which leads to a warped or one-sided understanding of the past. Therefore, skew precisely captures the harmful effect of such selective narration. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - → Option 1 – enrich means to improve or add value. Selective presentation does not improve understanding; it damages it. →Option 2 – legitimize means to make something appear acceptable or justified. The sentence talks about distortion, not validation. →Option 4 – preserve means to maintain in its original form. Selective narratives do the opposite by altering meaning.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. nuanced. 💢 Explanation - The sentence states: “Scholars therefore stress the need for a ____ interpretation of history that acknowledges complexity rather than glorifying a single perspective.” The key idea here is the recognition of multiple layers, subtleties, and complexities in historical understanding. Nuanced means characterized by subtle distinctions and careful consideration of different aspects of an issue. History is rarely black and white; a nuanced interpretation accepts contradictions, multiple viewpoints, and contextual factors, which aligns perfectly with the sentence. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 1 – unilateral means one-sided. The passage explicitly argues against a single perspective, so this option contradicts the meaning. → Option 3 – superficial means shallow or lacking depth. Scholars are calling for deeper, more thoughtful interpretation, not shallow analysis. →Option 4 – nostalgic means overly sentimental about the past. Sentimentality does not ensure accuracy or complexity in historical interpretation.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. against. 💢 Explanation - The sentence reads: “Only through such an approach can societies guard ____ cultural amnesia and sustain a balanced appreciation of their past.” The verb guard is commonly followed by the preposition against to mean protect from or prevent. The phrase guard against expresses taking precaution so that cultural amnesia (forgetting one’s heritage) does not occur. Hence, against is the grammatically correct and idiomatic choice. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - →Option 2 – with does not form a fixed collocation with guard and fails to convey prevention. →Option 3 – over is not used with guard in this sense. →Option 4 – by suggests agency or method, not protection from something.
